Because you got 28 and a half minutes less the three commercials you got to take out which generally are three two and one and a Half so you got so you got six and a half minutes out of the twenty eight and a half minutes that are gone for commercials You got to have 30 seconds front and back for the disclaimers and the notices So and then you got to divide that into three segments the first segment slightly longer than the set Then in the middle the middle slightly longer than the end and you really got to tell the entire sales story social Proof it and everything in each segment because nobody watches the thing from beginning to end they click in and out So long-form TV is really three segments of short-form TV Stitch together and now when somebody says to you you got a half an hour to present your DumaFludge you think wow half an hour. That's a lot of time Well, it isn't when you do to it what I just did and it's miserable because you're taping a testimonial and Charlie is using five words. He didn't need to use to tell that story and he's sucking up my clock and I know when we're in edit I'm gonna have to sacrifice something else if I'm gonna use what he just did But if I get him to tell it over and tell him to take those five words out He's gonna sound like he's reading from a script so I can't do that I can make him tell it over 16 times and hope he tells it shorter I can't but and then ultimately you're sitting there in an edit bay for four hours making a decision over three words and Three seconds. Well, what happens if we cut it this way? What happens if we cut it that way? They're horrible. I hate them and You get bad food. That's pizza and styrofoam cups are crap but so So I have this different like sense I think of Time and movement toward objectives than many people have and so the biggest thing I would say to you about the idea of Extreme productivity and its relationship to extreme income is that you have to put yourself under extreme pressure and Not have that pressure bother you but have it help you perform For pretty much every minute of every day and every meaningful measurement of movement towards your goals that exists in your business So just as an example I John and I were John Duquesne. I was just talking he had some direct sales experience too so when I moved from Ohio to Arizona I was in a direct sales business and So we were the business is trying to get people to perform in ways that they don't normally perform So you're trying to get you know people to get off their butt and use their spare time to run a second business And so when I moved to Arizona My anticipatory excitement having grown up here so here if you live in a Part of the country that is like Ohio here first of all now it wasn't different then but now Every weekend pretty much everybody spends their entire weekend driving back and forth from their home to home depot That's the weekend activity and They're sort of farmers who don't grow anything so they they spend their whole weekend Cutting grass and trimming shrubs and moving shit around and hammering this to that and sweating and you know and Everything that they're doing you can hire a kid to do for eight nine ten twelve bucks an hour Then in the winter they spend roughly 40% of their time shoveling snow So my and and this by the way you heard this see from people who Who who joined your business and said I want to make a million dollars? But I can't do anything this weekend because I got to cut the grass and trim the shrubs and hammer something to a tree and You just you wanted to hammer something you know into their into their hands And so my my anticipatory vision of moving to Arizona having grown up watching Roy Rogers movies and never having been there My anticipatory view of this was no grass no shrubs no trees no snow So no excuses right and nobody nobody's good. No nobody's gonna be doing any of this shit, right? So honest to God the first day I'm in Phoenix I'm driving around and if you've not been to Phoenix or Scottsdale There is there's no grass. There's hardly any shrubs for the most part So there's a entire neighborhoods where the entire lawn is gravel. It's desert, which is what it's supposed to be Desert that's like the point right there's supposed to be sand and an occasional cactus, right? The ones that have those yards they are out there raking the gravel They have what we call here a leaf rake because that's the fall excuse by the way is I got to break the leaves this weekend So I can't do anything so they're out there with leaf rakes raking the gravel into nice little
